text.title: Migration Phases
text.summary: Six sequential phases for large-scale PM tool migration, each with defined scope, duration guidance, and exit criteria.

rule.statement: Execute all six phases in order; skipping Preparation or Pilot is the leading cause of data-loss incidents at cutover.
rule.rationale: Phase 3 (Pilot) surfaces field-mapping failures on a small dataset at low cost; skipping it causes those failures to appear at Phase 4 scale with no rollback window.

list.item: Stakeholder alignment and sponsorship
Tool selection or evaluation (see pm-tools-comparison)
Migration scope definition — in-scope projects, date range, attachments threshold
Timeline creation with explicit buffer (50% over measured dry-run time)
Risk assessment: data loss, integration failure, adoption resistance
Target tool setup and admin configuration
Field mapping design — document every source field to target field, with fallback for unmapped
Migration script development (ETL engine — see content/02-etl-engine.xml)
Test environment creation for dry-run
Team communication: announce timeline, schedule training

rule.statement: Freeze the field mapping specification at end of Preparation; mid-migration mapping changes silently double scope.
rule.rationale: Each mapping change requires re-transforming and re-validating already-migrated records.

list.item: Select pilot project — representative complexity, NOT the simplest team
Execute migration with dry-run flag first, then live
Validate issue count, comment count, attachment byte count
Sample 50 items by hand before declaring pilot success
Gather feedback, adjust approach

antipattern.description: Picking the simplest team for the pilot project.
antipattern.reason: Creates false confidence; the simplest team has the fewest edge cases and the most forgiving stakeholders. Failures surface in Phase 4 at scale instead.

list.item: Execute in waves by team or project, not by record type
Validate each wave (three numbers: issues, comments, attachments-by-byte)
Address issues between waves; do not accumulate debt
Monitor rate limits; add 50% buffer to planned wave duration
T-24h: final export from source, backup target, notify all users
T-4h: set source to read-only, run final migration sync, validate counts
T-0: enable target for all users, send go-live announcement, support team on standby
T+1h: verify login success, automations running, address immediate issues
T+24h: review error logs, collect feedback, document known issues
T+1w: adoption metrics, complete missing migrations, retrospective, plan source decommission

rule.statement: Keep the source tool read-only for at least 30 days post-cutover; never delete it during stabilization.
rule.rationale: Teams reference historical context from source; premature deletion forces re-migration of data that was intentionally excluded.

list.item: Active support channel with named owner
Fix issues within 24 hours of report
Run training sessions for high-friction groups
Document lessons learned; schedule source decommission on a calendar date

rule.statement: Budget 2x the planned stabilization window; it always overruns.
rule.rationale: Edge cases and user adaptation issues accumulate in week 3-4 after initial excitement fades.
